  # Professor Richard Gilbertson
  
    + College positions:
        
        Master
    + Subjects: Medicine
  
  Richard Gilbertson completed his medical training and PhD in paediatric oncology in the UK before moving in 2000 to St Jude Children’s Research Hospital in the USA, where he served as Scientific and Comprehensive Cancer Centre Director, Executive Vice President and Lillian R Cannon Endowed Chair.
  
  In August 2015, he moved to the University of Cambridge where he is the Li Ka Shing Chair and Senior Group Leader at the CRUK Cambridge Institute, and was formerly Head of Department of Oncology and Director of the Cancer Centre. In October 2025 he was installed as Master of Gonville and Caius College.
  
  His laboratory research is focused on understanding the link between normal development and the origins of cancer, particularly childhood brain tumours. He is a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS), Fellow of the Academy of Medical Sciences UK (FMedSci), Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians, and member of the European Molecular Biology Organisation (EMBO).
  
    + Professor Peter Robinson
  
    + Home

  # Professor Peter Robinson
  
    + College positions:
        
        President
        
        Life Fellow
        
        Formerly Praelector, College Lecturer and Director of Studies in Computer Science
    + University positions:
        
        Professor of Computer Technology (Emeritus)
    + Subjects: Computer Science
  
  ### Degrees
  
  MA, PhD, ScD
  
  ### Awards and prizes
  
  Pilkington Prize for Teaching (1996)  
    
  IBM Faculty Award (2004)
  
  ### Research interests
  
  Computer graphics and interaction, investigating new technologies to enhance communication between computers and their users and new applications to exploit these technologies.
  
  ### Teaching interests
  
  Mathematics, computer graphics and image processing, innovative user interfaces.
  
  ### Other interests
  
  Christianity, family, hill walking, psephology, amateur radio.

# Professor Kay-Tee Khaw CBE

* College positions:
  
  Life Fellow, Jeffrey Cheah Professorial Fellow
  
  Equal Opportunities Officer
* University positions:
  
  Emeritus Professor of Clinical Gerontology
* Subjects: Medicine

### Qualifications

MA, MBBChir, University of Cambridge

MSc Epidemiology, London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine

FRCP Fellow Royal College of Physicians

FFPH Fellow of Faculty of Public Health

### Awards

FMedSci Fellow of the Academy of Medical Science 1999

Bisset Hawkins Trust Medal, Royal College of Physicians UK 2001

CBE Commander of the British Empire 2003

British Nutrition Foundation Medal 2007

NIHR Senior Investigator Award 2009-2018

World Heart Federation Award for outstanding contribution to Global Health 2020

### Research interests

Maintenance of health in later life and the causes and prevention of chronic diseases including cardiovascular disease, cancer and osteoporosis with a focus on nutrition and hormones. She is a principal investigator in the European Prospective Investigation in Cancer in Norfolk, part of a ten country half million participant research collaboration

### Teaching interests

Epidemiology, gerontology

### Other interests

Music

### Links

[http://www.epic-norfolk.org.uk](http://www.epic-norfolk.org.uk/ "http://www.epic-norfolk.org.uk")
